issues.description: Both endpoints (PATCH /hrmTimeTracking/guests and GET /hrmTimeTracking/guests/:guestId) lack any authentication guard. The documentation explicitly states 'Only authenticated members with appropriate permissions may access this endpoint. Guests themselves cannot list or search other guest records.' However, no @MemberAuth() or equivalent guard is applied. This allows unauthenticated callers to enumerate guest records including device fingerprints.
issues.suggestion: Add @MemberAuth() decorator to both the index() and at() methods, and pass the member payload to the provider for authorization enforcement.

issues.description: All three endpoints (POST, PATCH, GET) on /hrmTimeTracking/employees/:employeeId/snapshots lack any authentication guard. The documentation specifies employee:manage and employee:view permission requirements, but no @MemberAuth() or equivalent decorator is present. The authorization is delegated entirely to the provider layer with no controller-level enforcement.
issues.suggestion: Add @MemberAuth() decorator to all three methods (create, index, at) and pass the member payload to the providers so they can enforce permission checks.

issues.description: The provider fetches an employee record by ID without any organization-scoping or ownership validation. Any caller who knows an employee UUID can retrieve the full employee record regardless of organization membership. No authentication context is passed to this function.
issues.suggestion: Accept a member/auth context parameter and add a WHERE clause filtering by the authenticated member's organization ID (e.g., hrm_time_tracking_organization_id = member.organizationId) to enforce multi-tenant isolation.

issues.description: All controllers use console.log(error) for error handling, which may expose stack traces and internal details to logs without structured context. This is the standard AutoBE pattern but lacks structured logging with request context, severity levels, and correlation IDs.
issues.suggestion: Consider adopting a structured logging library (e.g., Winston, Pino) with log levels and request correlation IDs for production observability.

issues.description: No rate limiting is applied to any endpoint in this codebase. Guest enumeration and member endpoints are equally unprotected from brute-force or scraping attacks.
issues.suggestion: Apply rate limiting at the API gateway or using NestJS ThrottlerModule, especially on authentication and search endpoints.

issues.description: No authorization check before creating an employee snapshot. The function accepts a `member: IEntity` but never verifies that the member has permission to create snapshots for the given `employeeId`. Any authenticated member could create snapshots for any employee.
issues.suggestion: Before creating the snapshot, verify that the requesting member's employee record exists in the same organization as the target employee, and that they have the appropriate permission (e.g., 'employee:manage' or similar). Follow the pattern used in other providers like `putHrmTimeTrackingMemberDepartmentsDepartmentId`.

issues.description: No membership verification before returning organization file data. The function accepts `member` and `organizationId` but never checks that the requesting member is actually a member of the specified organization. Any authenticated member can retrieve files from any organization by guessing the organizationId.
issues.suggestion: Add a check to verify the requesting member has an active employee record in the specified organization before returning the file. Example: query `hrm_time_tracking_employees` where `hrm_time_tracking_member_id = member.id` and `hrm_time_tracking_organization_id = organizationId`.

issues.description: No membership verification before listing organization snapshots. The function scopes the query to `organizationId` but never verifies the requesting member belongs to that organization. Any authenticated member can query audit snapshots of any organization.
issues.suggestion: Add a membership check: verify the requesting member has an active employee record in the specified organization before executing the query.

issues.description: No authentication or authorization check in the delete contract function. The function takes `employeeId` and `contractId` but has no `member` parameter and performs no permission check. Any caller (if the controller passes through without a guard) can delete any employee's contract.
